title: Sentry quota watchdog (digest-cron piggyback)
problem: Sentry is the practice's only error-reporting processor, on the free plan (~5000 errors/month). A bad deploy that throws on every request burns the quota silently, and once it's exhausted Sentry DROPS events — the one tool that would flag the problem goes blind exactly when it matters. Nothing watched the quota. Closes prod-readiness gap #8.
analysis: Two pure helpers + a thin IO wrapper, same shape as the custodian reconciliation. evaluateQuota(used, quota, threshold) decides whether 30-day usage crossed the threshold (guards quota<=0 so a misconfig can't divide-by-zero into an alert storm). sumErrorQuantity(json) extracts the total from a Sentry stats_v2 response, tolerant of a missing/changed shape (returns 0 rather than throwing). checkSentryQuota(env) does the IO: skip when SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN/SENTRY_ORG unset, timed fetch of /organizations/{org}/stats_v2 (category=error, 30d), evaluate against SENTRY_MONTHLY_ERROR_QUOTA (default 5000), and write one admin_alerts row (kind sentry-quota) when over, deduped to one open alert. Runs from the daily digest cron as housekeeping #5.

decisions.decision: Configured monthly quota (default 5000) rather than introspecting Sentry's plan quota
decisions.rationale: Sentry's actual quota cap lives behind plan/subscription endpoints that vary by tier and are fragile to scrape. A single SENTRY_MONTHLY_ERROR_QUOTA var (defaulting to the free-plan 5000) is robust, obvious, and one edit away from matching a plan change.
decisions.recommendation: If the plan changes, update SENTRY_MONTHLY_ERROR_QUOTA in wrangler.toml [vars]. If Sentry ships a stable quota endpoint, swap the constant for a live read.

decisions.decision: Alert-only, deduped, on the digest cron — no dashboard, no new endpoint
decisions.rationale: Same reasoning as the custodian reconciliation: lowest surface, no new auth/CSRF path, and the daily digest is where the other housekeeping sweeps already live. One open alert at a time keeps the operator inbox honest.
decisions.recommendation: If quota pressure becomes a frequent, time-sensitive concern, add a /admin/today tile reading the same evaluation.

successCriteria:
- No-op (skipped:unconfigured) when SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN or SENTRY_ORG is unset
- usage >= 80% of quota → over, one admin_alerts row (kind sentry-quota)
- usage < 80% → no alert
- over while an open sentry-quota alert exists → no second row
- quota<=0 misconfig never reports over
- malformed Sentry response → 0 usage (treated as not-over), no crash
- a Sentry API failure must not fail the digest
implementation: functions/_lib/sentryQuota.ts (evaluateQuota + sumErrorQuantity pure, checkSentryQuota IO), wired in functions/api/admin/digest.ts as housekeeping #5. Env vars SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN (secret), SENTRY_ORG + SENTRY_MONTHLY_ERROR_QUOTA (wrangler.toml [vars]). Tests: functions/_lib/sentryQuota.test.ts (5 evaluate + 2 sum + 4 wrapper against D1Mock + a stubbed fetch).
userGuide: Runs automatically inside the daily digest cron once SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN + SENTRY_ORG are configured. When 30-day error usage crosses 80% of the monthly quota it writes an operator alert (kind sentry-quota) that surfaces in the digest email. Investigate the top issues in Sentry, then resolve the alert. See RUNBOOK §24.
knownLimitations: Quota cap is a configured constant, not read live from Sentry (deliberate — see decisions)
Threshold is a fixed 80%; not env-tunable (one constant change if it should be)
Counts category=error only — does not watch transaction/replay/attachment quotas
Requires a Sentry auth token with org:read; that token + the cron registration are manual setup steps

statusHistory.reason: Shipped as BACKLOG.md B9 / prod-readiness gap #8.
